repo_truth: Fresh read-only review of the exact five-file HCM-1.3 planning subject over baseline c5733785fbd60b7d7a19318cb86058395a02e1c3. HCM-1.1 and HCM-1.2 are reviewed dependency evidence. The packet makes the selected-profile artifact universe registry-owned while quarantining current pre-membrane Markdown product paths for HCM-1.4/HCM-2+, freezes one immutable engine owner API over unchanged HCM-1.2 profile/kind/descriptor/role/capability/validator/fingerprint owners, and permits no Rust in this session. Independently derive the verdict from live source and authority; do not inherit the parent's conclusion.
allowed_scope: Remain read-only and replay the sorted five-entry manifest and aggregate fingerprint before review.
Review every byte of the phase-map repair, SPEC, plan, unchecked todo, and planning proof wall for independent implementability and cross-document consistency.
Read unchanged control-pack authority, HCM-1.1/HCM-1.2 packets/closeouts, live HCM-1.2 source/tests/definitions/package proof/public APIs, and official dependency documentation only as needed.
Run read-only Markdown, link, Git, handoff/schema, source, package-topology, API feasibility, ordering, and security checks.
Return findings first with exact file/line evidence through the built-in channel.
non_goals: No edits, staging, commits, new dispatches, global handoffs, ledger writes, or user task hops.
No Rust, Cargo, runtime/product, setup/doctor, canonical YAML/content, HCM-1.4+, SDK, CLI, Tauri, Substrate, adapter, contract, or dock implementation.
Do not infer an unspecified schema, ID, fingerprint, default, compatibility, migration, fallback, product adoption, or later-slice behavior.
Do not require a preferred alternative unless the packet violates authority, an invariant, an exact boundary, implementability, security, or proof sufficiency.
tasks: Invoke the required skills in order, remain read-only, replay the exact five-entry manifest/aggregate, and return BLOCKED if any byte differs.

Review findings first in Critical, Required, Optional, Nit order. Every actionable finding must include severity, exact file/line, violated authority/invariant/gate, concrete failure, smallest valid remediation, and invalidated proof.

Verify the repaired phase status accurately records HCM-1.1/HCM-1.2 as landed dependency evidence, makes HCM-1.3 packet approval the next authorization boundary, and does not activate implementation or HCM-1.4.

Verify the exact HCM-1.3 owner boundary satisfies the phase row without secretly retaining the enum as selected-profile authority or inventing a legacy profile, dual dispatch, fallback, enum conversion, filename/command mapping, or product adoption.

Verify every consumed HCM-1.2 API and proposed public type/method/error is feasible on live source, exact enough to implement, ownership-safe, and does not duplicate or weaken profile/descriptor/schema/capability/fingerprint admission.

Verify kind and artifact-instance identities remain distinct; first-party roles/capabilities and custom kind/instance IDs bind without enum/CLI changes; all requiredness, dependencies, paths, structural validators, and semantic validator metadata are data-driven.

Verify the construction/fail-fast algorithms and providers-before-consumers ordering are internally correct, deterministic, and fully specified for instance and capability dependencies, multiple providers, lexical ties, empty dependency sets, and retained unused kinds.

Verify trusted repo-relative path/source/identity/fingerprint/duplicate/compatibility/dependency boundaries are preserved exactly, new registry errors cannot leak host paths, and no unjustified numeric limit or executable semantic hook is introduced.
Verify the 23-file live fixed-consumer inventory is complete and each file has a precise HCM-1.3 or later-slice disposition consistent with HCM-1.4 setup/doctor and HCM-2/HCM-3 ownership.

Verify exact allowed existing files/new areas/symbol ownership, task order, TDD red-green increments, literal positives, N/N+1 boundaries, negative/security/regression/Windows/package/scope proof, classification ceiling, exit gate, and stop conditions make a future implementation separable.
Verify package proof uses literal member set equality plus per-member hash/size against HCM-1.2 and that no count-only proof or test fixture becomes package authority.
Verify SPEC, plan, todo, proof wall, and phase map agree on every owner, file, task, proof, non-goal, review loop, two-commit closeout, and terminal stop.
Verify planning stayed docs-only, todo is entirely unchecked, no coupled control-pack file was omitted, and the proof wall honestly distinguishes pre-review proof from final review/closeout evidence.
Return CLEAN only if there is no valid Critical or Required finding; otherwise return CHANGES_REQUIRED or BLOCKED. Make no edits.
stop_conditions: Return BLOCKED if the manifest/aggregate, required authority, live source, or mandatory read-only verification cannot be reproduced.
Return CHANGES_REQUIRED rather than filling a missing schema, identifier, fingerprint, default, compatibility, migration, fallback, product-adoption, or later-slice decision by inference.
Return to the parent if remediation would require Rust/runtime work, broader control-pack authority, HCM-1.4+, or a global handoff/ledger mutation.
